<source_slice path="logstash-core/src/main/java/org/logstash/common/BufferedTokenizerExt.java" lines="81-123">
81:     @SuppressWarnings("rawtypes")
82:     public RubyArray extract(final ThreadContext context, IRubyObject data) {
83:         final RubyArray entities = data.convertToString().split(delimiter, -1);
84:         if (hasSizeLimit) {
85:             final int entitiesSize = ((RubyString) entities.first()).size();
86:             if (inputSize + entitiesSize > sizeLimit) {
87:                 throw new IllegalStateException("input buffer full");
88:             }
89:             this.inputSize = inputSize + entitiesSize;
90:         }
91:         input.append(entities.shift(context));
92:         if (entities.isEmpty()) {
93:             return RubyUtil.RUBY.newArray();
94:         }
95:         entities.unshift(input.join(context));
96:         input.clear();
97:         input.append(entities.pop(context));
98:         inputSize = ((RubyString) input.first()).size();
99:         return entities;
100:     }
101: 
102:     /**
103:      * Flush the contents of the input buffer, i.e. return the input buffer even though
104:      * a token has not yet been encountered
105:      *
106:      * @param context ThreadContext
107:      * @return Buffer contents
108:      */
109:     @JRubyMethod
110:     public IRubyObject flush(final ThreadContext context) {
111:         final IRubyObject buffer = input.join(context);
112:         input.clear();
113:         inputSize = 0;
114:         return buffer;
115:     }
116: 
117:     @JRubyMethod(name = "empty?")
118:     public IRubyObject isEmpty(final ThreadContext context) {
119:         return RubyUtil.RUBY.newBoolean(input.isEmpty() && (inputSize == 0));
120:     }
121: 
122: }
123:

84:     @SuppressWarnings("rawtypes")
85:     private static RubyArray deepList(final Ruby runtime, final Collection<?> list) {
86:         final int length = list.size();
87:         final RubyArray array = runtime.newArray(length);
88:         for (final Object item : list) {
89:             array.add(deep(runtime, item));
90:         }
91:         return array;
92:     }
93: 
94:     private static RubyHash deepMap(final Ruby runtime, final Map<?, ?> map) {
95:         final RubyHash hash = RubyHash.newHash(runtime);
96:         // Note: RubyHash.put calls JavaUtil.convertJavaToUsableRubyObject on keys and values
97:         map.forEach((key, value) -> hash.put(key, deep(runtime, value)));
98:         return hash;
99:     }

38:     @SuppressWarnings("unchecked")
39:     public static <T> T deep(final T input) {
40:         if (input instanceof Map<?, ?>) {
41:             return (T) deepMap((Map<?, ?>) input);
42:         } else if (input instanceof List<?>) {
43:             return (T) deepList((List<?>) input);
44:         } else if (input instanceof RubyString) {
45:             // new instance but sharing ByteList (until either String is modified)
46:             return (T) ((RubyString) input).dup();
47:         } else if (input instanceof Collection<?>) {
48:             throw new ClassCastException("unexpected Collection type " + input.getClass());
49:         }
50: 
51:         return input;
52:     }
53: 
54:     private static <E> List<E> deepList(final List<E> list) {
55:         List<E> clone;
56:         if (list instanceof LinkedList<?>) {
57:             clone = new LinkedList<>();
58:         } else if (list instanceof ArrayList<?>) {
59:             clone = new ArrayList<>();
60:         } else {
61:             throw new ClassCastException("unexpected List type " + list.getClass());
62:         }
63: 
64:         for (E item : list) {
65:             clone.add(deep(item));
66:         }
